sourceText: This emphasizes the importance of critical thinking and embracing doubt with an open mind in the pursuit of truth.
Chronic illnesses, neuralgia from a young age, and profound religious and philosophical introspection caused him significant inner turmoil.
Despite physical suffering, he relentlessly pursued intellectual inquiry, transforming his pain into an object of faith and seeking spiritual solace.
It means that although humans are fragile and easily broken beings, their capacity for thought makes them the noblest beings in the universe.
The heart has its reasons which reason knows nothing of.
This suggests that human emotions and intuition possess a profound realm that cannot be explained by logic and reason alone.
All men are subject to all evils. All men are deceived.
This is an insight into human nature, suggesting that humans tend to deceive themselves, unable to face their own weakness and limitations.
Poverty, the absence of his father, academic limitations due to his non-noble status, and the skeptical gaze and lack of recognition from peers regarding his initial periodic table.
Overcame adversities through relentless research and dedication, driven by an obsession to classify elements, ultimately achieving recognition for his monumental work.
Scientific inquiry begins with constant questioning and critical thinking.
